description:
You are the configuration manager for a small research project that benchmarks
    different optimisation solvers.  
    A Git repository is already initialised in the directory
    /home/user/projects/optimize_app

    1) Inside that directory there is currently a single configuration file

          /home/user/projects/optimize_app/solver_config.yaml

       with the *exact* contents shown below (including all spaces and the
       trailing newline at the end of the last line):

            solver:
              name: "glpk"
              tolerance: 1e-6
              max_iterations: 1000
              verbose: false

    2) There is **no** change–tracking log yet.  
       Your task is to create one **and** to upgrade the solver settings.

    3) Required end-state:

       a) The original configuration must be kept as a backup file named

            /home/user/projects/optimize_app/solver_config_2023-01-01T00:00:00Z.bak

          The backup file must be an *exact* byte-for-byte copy of the original
          solver_config.yaml shown above.

       b) Update /home/user/projects/optimize_app/solver_config.yaml
          so that it contains *exactly* (including spaces, indentation and the
          trailing newline) the following:

              solver:
                name: "cbc"
                tolerance: 1e-8
                max_iterations: 1000
                verbose: true
                time_limit: 300

       c) Create the new log file

            /home/user/projects/optimize_app/config_changes.log

          and append **one** single-line record that tracks the change.  
          The line must have *precisely* five pipe-separated fields
          and must **not** contain any extra spaces.  
          Use the fixed timestamp 2023-01-01T00:00:00Z (do NOT use the current
          time).  The required format is:

            YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ|<sha256_old>|<sha256_new>|user:automation-bot|comment:"Upgrade solver to CBC with tighter tolerance and time limit"

          • Field 1  – literal        2023-01-01T00:00:00Z  
          • Field 2  – the lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the backup file  
          • Field 3  – the lowercase hexadecimal SHA-256 digest of the **updated**
                       solver_config.yaml  
          • Field 4  – literal        user:automation-bot  
          • Field 5  – literal        comment:"Upgrade solver to CBC with tighter tolerance and time limit"

          Example of the *shape* (hashes will differ):

            2023-01-01T00:00:00Z|6f5902ac237024bdd0c176cb93063dc4|5feceb66ffc86f38d952786c6d696c79|user:automation-bot|comment:"Upgrade solver to CBC with tighter tolerance and time limit"

          The line must end with a single newline character.

       d) Stage and commit the three files
             solver_config.yaml  
             solver_config_2023-01-01T00:00:00Z.bak  
             config_changes.log  
          with the commit message

             feat: switch to CBC solver

    4) Verification notes (information for you, **not** part of the log):

       • The SHA-256 digests will be recomputed by the automated tests and must
         exactly match the contents of the second and third fields.  
       • The only acceptable delimiter inside config_changes.log is the pipe
         character “|”.  No surrounding spaces are allowed.  
       • The log file must contain exactly **one** line.  
       • The three files *must* be present in the last commit.

    IMPORTANT: do not use sudo – your account already owns all files.
               Make sure every command finishes without an error before moving
               on to the next one.
truth:
<!--
        Ground-truth data used by the automated checker.
        The checker will verify:

        1) /home/user/projects/optimize_app/solver_config.yaml
           contains EXACTLY (byte-for-byte):

              solver:
                name: "cbc"
                tolerance: 1e-8
                max_iterations: 1000
                verbose: true
                time_limit: 300
              \n

        2) /home/user/projects/optimize_app/solver_config_2023-01-01T00:00:00Z.bak
           contains EXACTLY (byte-for-byte):

              solver:
                name: "glpk"
                tolerance: 1e-6
                max_iterations: 1000
                verbose: false
              \n

        3) The SHA-256 digests that appear in fields 2 and 3 of
           /home/user/projects/optimize_app/config_changes.log

             Field 2 must equal sha256sum of the .bak file above.
             Field 3 must equal sha256sum of the updated solver_config.yaml.

        4) The log file path:
/home/user/projects/optimize_app/config_changes.log

           contains precisely ONE line with the five pipe-separated fields:
           '2023-01-01T00:00:00Z|<digest_old>|<digest_new>|user:automation-bot|comment:"Upgrade solver to CBC with tighter tolerance and time limit"\n'

        5) Git repository in /home/user/projects/optimize_app has HEAD commit
           whose message is exactly "feat: switch to CBC solver"
           and whose tree contains the three files specified above.
    -->
